context: Tests whether the agent generates tasks that are fully traceable to the plan and spec: file paths match the plan's project structure, user story tags match spec stories, TS-XXX references are comma-separated (not ranges), and no tasks reference technologies or files not defined in the plan.

checklist.name: File paths match plan structure
checklist.description: Task descriptions reference file paths that exist in the plan's Project Structure section (e.g., src/services/event-service.ts, src/routes/events.ts, prisma/schema.prisma). No invented paths like src/controllers/ or src/utils/ that aren't in the plan

checklist.name: Every user story has tagged tasks
checklist.description: All three user stories (US-1, US-2, US-3) have at least one task tagged with [US1], [US2], [US3] respectively

checklist.name: Setup/Foundational tasks have no story tags
checklist.description: Tasks in Setup and Foundational phases do NOT have [USn] labels — these are shared infrastructure, not story-specific

checklist.name: TS references are comma-separated
checklist.description: When tasks reference multiple test specs, they use comma-separated format like [TS-003, TS-004, TS-005] — NOT prose ranges like 'TS-003 through TS-005' or 'TS-003 to TS-005'

checklist.name: TS references match provided .feature files
checklist.description: TS-XXX references in tasks correspond to actual @TS-XXX tags in the provided .feature files (TS-001 through TS-007). No references to TS-008 or higher that don't exist

checklist.name: Priority ordering respected
checklist.description: P1 user story tasks (US-1 Create Event, US-2 Purchase Ticket) appear before P2 tasks (US-3 View Listings) in the phase structure

checklist.name: Phase structure complete
checklist.description: tasks.md has all required phases: Setup (project init, schema), Foundational (shared models, database), User Story phases (by priority), and a Polish/Final phase

checklist.name: [P] markers only on parallelizable tasks
checklist.description: [P] markers appear only on tasks that can genuinely run in parallel (different files, no mutual dependencies). Tasks that depend on each other (e.g., model before service) are NOT marked [P]

checklist.name: No technologies beyond the plan
checklist.description: Tasks do not introduce frameworks, libraries, or tools not mentioned in the plan (e.g., no Redis, no GraphQL, no React — the plan specifies Express, Prisma, Vitest, Resend)

checklist.name: Checkbox format used
checklist.description: All tasks use the markdown checkbox format: - [ ] TNNN description

checklist.name: Sequential T-prefixed IDs
checklist.description: Tasks use sequential zero-padded IDs: T001, T002, T003, etc. with no gaps or duplicates
